Let me get specific here just in terms of prospecting. I'm going to tell you exactly what prospecting is all about and what it is that you're actually going to be doing here. But here's the thing, prospector is real prospectors, like gold prospectors. They do this alluvial mining in the stream where they're actually sifting for gold in a stream. They're sifting, sitting there with a pen, they're sifting through countless thousands and thousands and hundreds of thousands of gallons of water and they're sifting and they're sifting and they're sifting through the water and they're looking for that one nugget of gold made like those countless thousands of gallons of water, right? What they're not doing is they're not sifting through the water and hoping that if they go through enough water they'll eventually turn the water into gold. You understand that? You're not looking to turn water into gold. We're not alchemists. We're salespeople. We're influences. We're visionaries. When you're prospecting, the biggest mistake that you can make is you try to use this sort of influence and tonal and all this other stuff that I'm teaching you to turn non-buyers into buyers. There's people that would have never bought anyway, just going to force them into the system, right? You end up wasting your time, you load your pipeline with crap, then you start to think, you know, geez, I don't understand. I did all the straight line stuff and it didn't work. I didn't close people. The reason is because they were never buyers in the first place. Listen, at my brokerage room when I had these guys training in the straight line system, they were masters of it to the highest level. They can close pretty much anyone who was closeable. They were so well trained, but guess what? You know what their percentage of people that would close? It would be one out of a hundred people they'd speak to. That was the actual percentage of closing. They might go through 250 leads and get from those 200 leads, maybe 15 or 20 people that wanted to hear back from them again, and get maybe just one or two people at the end of the day, but from there, those numbers justified a massive, massive firm behind it. So again, it's not about you have to speak to everybody and close everybody. Everyone is not closeable. Most people in fact who you speak to should probably be weeded out. And it's the ones that actually do qualify that answer the questions in the way that actually says, yeah, okay, they can use my product, they need my product, and they're financially qualified. Bam, those are the ones that you bring down the pipeline through the straight line. Now there are two sides of the equation here in most companies. You have marketing on one side and sales on the other. Marketing is what brings people into your sales funnel, and the sales is what actually closes people, what actually gets the order. So imagine we have a marketing campaign that's great and it's bringing in lots and lots of people, and of course you want all these people to be as qualified as possible, but usually no matter how good any marketing program is, they're not going to be 100% qualified. So what you do then is you prospect, you go through these people, they enter your sales funnel, and you sift out the ones who are potential buyers, your so-called nuggets of gold, the same way a prospector was sifting through water to get his nuggets of gold.
